source: Official GGG mechanic statements and official forum Q&A

Definition.text: Spell is a skill class distinct from Attack; it defines spell-classified skill delivery and spell-tagged effect scaling.
Definition.source: pathofexile.com forum post Mark_GGG on mechanics thread (Nov 2011)

Purpose.text: Route skill effects, scaling, and modifiers through spell-specific mechanics and validation paths.
Purpose.source: Official GGG terminology in mechanic responses

Core Rules.text: A skill follows Spell mechanics when it is Spell-tagged; Spell skills are not attacks unless an effect explicitly defines attack-based sub-components.

Internal Rules.text: Spell modifiers are evaluated through the Spell pipeline and are orthogonal to base damage type; physical, elemental, and other damage types do not redefine Spell classification.
Internal Rules.source: pathofexile.com forum Mechanics thread and Mark_GGG quote

Calculation Order.text: 1) spell invocation 2) hit/defer checks if applicable 3) damage-type processing 4) spell-specific modifiers and secondary effects.
Calculation Order.source: mechanic classification language in official and community-anchored documentation

Priority Rules.text: Specific exceptions (e.g., skill component that does not use weapon damage) override generic class assumptions.
Priority Rules.source: Mark_GGG response on explosive/ infernal blow special cases

Preconditions.text: Skill must be a spell-class action and follow spell validation conditions for targeted application and hit checks.

Postconditions.text: Spell Damage modifiers can apply even when base damage type is physical or another non-spell-type group.
Postconditions.source: detonate dead example in Mechanics thread

Limitations.text: Being Spell does not imply specific damage type or targeting geometry.

Caps.text: No generic cap for Spell class as a keyword in this source set.

Formula.text: No standalone mathematical formula for the Spell keyword itself; modifiers are consumed by stat stack pipeline.

cannot_affect: spells explicitly excluded by skill text

text: Spell tag assumed on mixed damage
Attack skills may include components whose components are spell damage (e.g., explosion components on specific attacks).

Hidden Rules.text: Store Spell class as orthogonal to damage-type tags in schema; do not infer physical/cold/fire/etc from spell-ness.

Developer Notes.text: When a mechanic applies to both attack and spell, use explicit dual wording like 'with spells and attacks' or global scope.

Historical Changes.text: No explicit version-scoped Spell-redefinition passage found in this batch.

text.note: Spell remains defined by skill class and non-weapon application path.

text: Detonate Dead is a spell that deals physical damage; it is affected by spell damage modifiers, not elemental-only modifiers.
Explosive/Infernal Blow can include spell-damage explosion components while the base skill is an attack.
All spell effects are affected by attack checks
